---
title: "Python Tip: f-strings"
subtitle: "Cleaner, faster, and more readable string formatting"
author: "Shep Bryan IV"
description: "Learn how to use f-strings for cleaner, faster, and more readable string formatting with precision control."
---



<!-- PANEL 1 -->
:::{.tutorial-panel}

# Python Tip: f-strings

Tired of complex string formatting?
Use f-strings for cleaner, faster, and more readable code.

:::



<!-- PANEL 2 -->
:::{.tutorial-panel}

## Basic f-strings

F-strings (formatted string literals) allow you to quickly insert variables into strings.
Just put an `f` before the string and use curly braces to insert variables.

:::{.code-animation}
```{python}
name = "Bob"
age = 30

# Don't do this
mystring = "Hello, " + name + "! You are " + str(age) + " years old."
print(mystring)

# Do this instead
mystring = f"Hello, {name}! You are {age} years old."
print(mystring)
```
:::

:::



<!-- PANEL 3 -->
:::{.tutorial-panel}

## Formatting with Precision

You can format numbers with precision using f-strings. Simply add a colon, a number, and a letter to the variable.

:::{.code-animation}
```{python}
# Format float decimal places with `f`
pi = 3.141592653589793
print(f"π ≈ {pi:.2f}")  # 2 decimal places
print(f"π ≈ {pi:.4f}")  # 4 decimal places
```
:::

:::{.code-animation}
```{python}
# Format significant figures with `g`
mass_electron = 9.10938356e-31
print(f"m ≈ {mass_electron:.2g}")  # 2 significant figures
print(f"m ≈ {mass_electron:.4g}")  # 4 significant figures
```
:::

:::{.code-animation}
```{python}
# Format integer with padding with `d`
print(f"Answer = {42:02d}")  # Pad to 2 digits
print(f"Answer = {42:04d}")  # Pad to 4 digits
```
:::

:::{.code-animation}
```{python}
# Format dates with `:format_spec`
from datetime import datetime
print(f"Today: {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d}")
```
:::

:::



<!-- PANEL 4 -->
:::{.tutorial-panel}

## Wrap-Up

Now you can use f-strings to format strings with ease, precision, and readability.

Follow me for more tips.  
Shep Bryan IV

:::
